commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.1: the specimen — the gallery title wearing many faces (#24)

Reskin the Forms gallery's cascade title node into a TYPE-SPECIMEN PLATE that
performs its own sentence: each glyph of 'The same idea, / wearing many faces.'
auditions many REAL faces — the page's three loaded families (serif display /
sans / mono) plus a Fraunces opsz+wght+slant morph — and settles, left to right
with scroll, onto the house engraved cut. Many letters mid-audition at once IS
'wearing many faces'; the settle IS 'one idea'.

Chrome: matte jade crop-mark corners + a tracked catalogue label (SPECIMEN · THE
FORMS) — a print specimen sheet, deliberately distinct from Observe's glowing CRT
scope, the terminal window, and the framed bloom box. This seeds the gallery's
cohesive vocabulary (parent aj1.7) without touching any sibling painter.

- The look lives entirely in the cascade painter + its .cs-* CSS block, selected
  by node.kind; the shared paint loop stays generic. [LAW:locality-or-seam]
- The face is a pure function of per-glyph scroll progress — fully scrubbable and
  reversible, no timers. [LAW:no-ambient-temporal-coupling]
- Each cell's width is locked to its SETTLED metric once fonts.ready fires (the
  draw painter's lazy-measure idiom), so family/axis swaps restyle in place and
  never reflow the centred line — settled text is rock-steady.
- Honest chrome: the catalogue label is static identity, no fabricated metric to
  drift. [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
- REDUCE_MOTION (and the pre-lock frames) collapse to a calm opacity-only reveal;
  the kinetic audition is fully gated, matching the other painters.

Verified in Chrome across the node's full scroll range: letters audition faces
mid-sweep, settle to a steady engraved house face, reduced-motion gates the
flourish; zero page console errors.
